# terrible_community_aws_application_autoscaling_policy (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against
- `policy_name` (String) The name of the scaling policy.
- `policy_type` (String) The policy type.
- `resource_id` (String) The identifier of the resource associated with the scalable target.
- `scalable_dimension` (String) The scalable dimension associated with the scalable target.
- `service_namespace` (String) The namespace of the AWS service.
- `state` (String) Whether a policy should be `present` or `absent`.

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `maximum_tasks` (Number) The maximum value to scale to in response to a scale out event. This parameter is required if you are creating a first new policy for the specified service.
- `minimum_tasks` (Number) The minimum value to scale to in response to a scale in event. This parameter is required if you are creating a first new policy for the specified service.
- `override_task_capacity` (Boolean) Whether or not to override values of minimum and/or maximum tasks if it's already set. Defaults to `false`.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `step_scaling_policy_configuration` (String) A step scaling policy. This parameter is required if you are creating a policy and *policy_type=StepScaling*.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `target_tracking_scaling_policy_configuration` (String) A target tracking policy. This parameter is required if you are creating a new policy and *policy_type=TargetTrackingScaling*. Full documentation of the suboptions can be found in the API documentation: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/autoscaling/application/APIReference/API_TargetTrackingScalingPolicyConfiguration.html
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution

### Read-Only

- `alarms` (String) List of the CloudWatch alarms associated with the scaling policy
- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `creation_time` (String) The Unix timestamp for when the scalable target was created.
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
- `max_capacity` (Number) The maximum value to scale to in response to a scale out event. Required if *state* is `present`.
- `min_capacity` (Number) The minimum value to scale to in response to a scale in event. Required if *state* is `present`.
- `policy_arn` (String) The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the scaling policy..
- `role_arn` (String) The ARN of an IAM role that allows Application Auto Scaling to modify the scalable target on your behalf. Required if *state* is `present`.
